title: 10 best herbal teas for excessive sweating

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle teas helps with excessive sweating because of its unique ability to balance body temperature. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce heat stress, which is often a contributing factor to excessive sweating. Additionally, milk thistle's silymarin compound has been shown to improve blood circulation and detoxification processes, allowing the body to better regulate its natural cooling mechanisms. By promoting a sense of calm and reducing overall bodily inflammation, milk thistle teas can help alleviate excessive sweating episodes.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to excessive sweating.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in milk thistle tea have a cooling effect on the body, helping to regulate body temperature and reduce sweat production.

Lactucopicrin: Lactucopicrin is a sesquiterpene lactone that has been shown to inhibit the activity of the sympathetic nervous system, which can help calm down excessive sweating caused by anxiety or stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and silymarin, which help to reduce sweat production and alleviate associated symptoms.
Seeds: Containing saponins and phytosterols, these seeds have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh milk thistle flowers and leaves and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Chop the fresh milk thistle into small pieces using scissors or a knife to release their medicinal properties.
Combine one tablespoon of chopped milk thistle with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the hot water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids before serving hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle teas helps with excessive sweating because its natural antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties help to regulate body temperature, reducing the production of sweat. The tea's ability to calm the nervous system also reduces stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for excessive sweating. Additionally, stinging nettle has been shown to improve circulation, which can help to reduce hot flashes and night sweats associated with menopause and other hormonal imbalances. By promoting a sense of relaxation and balance in the body, stinging nettle teas can provide relief from excessive sweating.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Stinging nettle tea contains a high amount of antioxidants, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, leading to a decrease in excessive sweat production.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Nettle tea is rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which help soothe skin irritation and reduce sweating caused by conditions like hyperhidrosis.

Sweat-diminishing flavonoids: Specific flavonoids present in nettle tea, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to directly inhibit sweat production, reducing excessive sweating and promoting a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiperspirant properties, helping to reduce sweat production.
Rhizomes: High in bioactive compounds like scopoletin, which has a calming effect on the sweat glands, reducing excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves in the morning when they are most potent and free of dew.
Rinse the nettle leaves thoroughly with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of dried or 2-3 tablespoons of fresh stinging nettle leaves with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes, allowing the tea to cool and infuse with the herbal properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids before serving immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort teas helps with excessive sweating because it is a natural antiperspirant that reduces the body's ability to produce sweat. The active compound hypericin in the tea works by blocking the neurotransmitter acetylcholine, which is responsible for stimulating the sweat glands. As a result, St John's Wort tea can effectively reduce sweating in individuals with hyperhidrosis, providing relief from embarrassing and uncomfortable excessive sweating episodes. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has antiperspirant properties, which help to reduce sweat production by blocking the sweat glands and decreasing the activity of neurotransmitters that stimulate sweating.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort tea have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation caused by excessive sweating.
Hypoallergenic compounds: St. John's Wort contains hypoallergenic compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate allergic reactions that may be contributing to excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to reduce sweat production and alleviate symptoms of excessive sweating.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming the skin affected by excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried hypericum perforatum flowers into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Boil fresh water and pour it over the dried st john's wort flowers in the cup or teapot allowing for a 5-minute steeping time.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth into another cup or container discarding the solids.
Add honey to taste and stir well to dissolve the sweetener in the herbal tea taking approximately 2-3 minutes.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming it within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with excessive sweating because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce body temperature and soothe the nervous system. The diuretic properties of dandelion tea also help to eliminate excess fluids from the body, reducing sweat production and promoting a sense of dryness and comfort. Additionally, dandelion tea's ability to balance blood sugar levels can also contribute to reduced sweating by regulating the body's hormonal responses to temperature changes. Overall, dandelion tea offers a natural and effective solution for managing excessive sweating.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion tea helps increase urine production, which can reduce overall sweat production by flushing out toxins and excess fluids from the body.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in dandelion tea can help relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms that contribute to excessive sweating.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce inflammation in the sweat glands, which may contribute to excessive sweating, and also alleviate related skin irritations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, dandelion roots help to calm and regulate sweat glands.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, dandelion buds support immune function and reduce inflammation associated with excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried dandelion roots and flowers from your local herbalist or health food store weighing approximately 2 ounces.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 5 minutes.
Combine one teaspoon of ground dandelion root with one cup of boiling water in a teapot and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids immediately after preparation.
Drink two to three cups of the herbal dandelion tea per day, ideally between meals, for optimal benefits and results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Danshen teas helps with excessive sweating because it contains salvanolic acid, a compound that has natural antiperspirant properties. This acid works to reduce the production of sweat by blocking the activation of eccrine glands, which are responsible for producing sweat. Additionally, danshen tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects help to soothe the skin and reduce redness and irritation often associated with excessive sweating. As a result, danshen teas can provide relief from unwanted sweat and its accompanying discomforts.
remedy_properties: Diterpenoid compounds: Danshen teas contain diterpenoid compounds, such as tanshinone IIA, which have been shown to reduce sweat production by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in sweat gland function.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Danshen teas help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to excessive sweating, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that may exacerbate sweating conditions.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Danshen's anti-inflammatory properties can help to alleviate symptoms of excessive sweating by reducing inflammation in the sweat glands and surrounding tissues, which can reduce sweat production and improve overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make teas that help to regulate body temperature and reduce excessive sweating by its cooling and calming effects.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe skin irritation caused by excessive sweating and reduce sweat production.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried danshen root for every eight ounces of water.
Boil the measured water in a pot and let it cool down for at least five minutes.
Add the dried danshen root to the cooled-down boiling water and let it steep for seven to nine minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug and discard solids.
Drink the danshen tea immediately after straining while still warm, ideally within thirty seconds of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats teas helps with excessive sweating because of its unique composition. The beta-glucans present in oatmeal have natural antiperspirant properties, which help to absorb moisture and reduce sweat production. remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Oat tea contains antioxidants like avenanthramides, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, thereby reducing sweat production.
Soothing astringent compounds: Oat tea contains astringent compounds like saponins and flavonoids, which help dry out the skin and reduce moisture levels, making it less prone to excessive sweating.

Antimicrobial properties: Oat tea has antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to excessive sweating, such as sweat-induced fungal rashes or skin irritations.

remedy_recipe: Boil one teaspoon of rolled oats in two cups of water for five minutes to infuse the beneficial compounds.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large bowl to remove any solid particles.
Add one teaspoon of dried sage leaves and let it steep for an additional three minutes to enhance sweat reduction properties.
Remove from heat and stir in one tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea and improve palatability, taking five minutes to allow the mixture to cool.
Strain the tea again through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discard the solids, and serve warm or chilled as desired, consuming two cups throughout the day.

remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality paeonia lactiflora roots dried or fresh and measure out 20-30 grams for every 8 ounces of water.
Combine the measured paeonia lactiflora roots with cold water in a saucepan and allow it to steep for at least 6 hours.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large pot or teapot and discard the solids.
Bring the strained tea to a rolling boil over high heat then reduce heat to medium-low for 10-15 minutes of gentle simmering.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving hot or letting it steep in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours for chilled consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with excessive sweating because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which in turn reduces stress and anxiety that can trigger excessive sweating. The herb's sedative properties help to relax the body, allowing for better regulation of body temperature, which minimizes sweat production. Additionally, valerian tea's ability to promote deep sleep can also help alleviate nighttime sweating associated with hot flashes or night sweats. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, valerian teas provide a natural solution for excessive sweating.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian tea's sedative properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for excessive sweating, allowing the body to regulate its natural sweat response.

Antispasmodic properties: Valerian tea's ability to relax muscles can help alleviate muscle spasms and cramps that may contribute to excessive sweating, such as those experienced by people with hyperhidrosis (excessive sweating disorder).

Adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian tea's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress and anxiety, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and valepotriates, which help to reduce sweat gland activity and alleviate symptoms of hyperhidrosis.
Rhizomes: Contain a unique combination of essential oils that have been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Cut the cleaned valeriana officinalis roots into small pieces to facilitate efficient brewing.
Combine one teaspoon of dried or two teaspoons of fresh valeriana officinalis roots with one cup boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active ingredients to infuse and combine.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids before drinking immediately.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for excessive sweating are a natural and effective solution to manage hyperhidrosis, a condition characterized by excessive sweating. These teas contain ingredients that help reduce sweat production and alleviate anxiety related to sweating. Examples of herbal teas that help with excessive sweating include peppermint tea, sage tea, and green tea, which have been shown to decrease sweat glands' activity and improve overall well-being. By consuming these teas, individuals can reduce their sweat levels, regain confidence, and live a more comfortable life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat excessive sweating with herbal teas is by brewing and consuming a blend of cooling herbs like peppermint, basil, and lemongrass. These herbs have natural astringent properties that help reduce sweat and body temperature. Another effective option is using sage tea, which has antiperspirant properties that help regulate sweat glands. Drinking 2-3 cups of these teas daily can provide relief from excessive sweating.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to excessive sweating that are treated with herbal teas are anxiety, stress, and hot flashes. Herbs like passionflower, chamomile, and lemon balm help calm the nervous system and reduce symptoms of anxiety and stress. Teas made from herbs like black cohosh, red clover, and St. John's Wort can alleviate hot flashes associated with menopause. These teas work by regulating hormones, promoting relaxation, and reducing inflammation, providing a natural alternative to traditional medications.
